Petition For Allowance Of Extraordinary Fees Regarding Missing Heir, Mark Portillo
Off Calendar Nov-17-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Roddick, via ZOOM, in propria persona, Petitioner.

The court asks counsel if Mark checked himself in to a facility. Counsel reports that he is unsure of the preceding. The court then asks if Mark was in a facility in 2016. Counsel reports that he believes so. The court asks if he ever received return mail from the facility. Counsel reports that he has and reports that he doesn't remember when that was exactly but that the matter had already resolved. The court then confirms that counsel's former client was Jeff Riggler who is Mark's uncle, Kim Portillo's brother. The court and counsel discuss his request for fees which seem excessive to the court. Counsel provides oral testimony in support of his petition including the complex nature of this matter and that he is a solo practitioner and does all the work himself.

The Petition for Allowance of Extraordinary Fees, etc., is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-24-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Roddick, Esq., counsel for and with Jeffrey Rigler, Executor (appearing by telephone).

The Court recites the matters before the Court and questions the extraordinary fees requested for litigation attorneys in the negotiated buyout of the property involving four different owners, one of whom was the personal representative, Mr. Rigler. The Court expresses concern that the litigation was not for the benefit of the estate but rather, for the benefit of Mr. Rigler individually. Mr. Roddick addresses the Court's concerns with limited satisfaction.

The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Ruiz; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Roddick for Jeffrey Rigler (Administrator).

Mr. Roddick requested a 30-day continuance stating that they finally sold the property, that he is waiting for the January statement and that he will file a final accounting. Status on close of estate is continued to March 2, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the matter is not ready for final distribution, Mr. Roddick is to file a petition for approval of report on status of administration. Either petition is to be filed by February 24, 2020.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Anasse. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar May-22-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Roddick for and with Jeffrey Rigler

The Court sought clarification regarding the intended date of death. Counsel and Mr. Rigler spoke of the circumstances regarding Ms. Portillo's reported missing dates. The Petition for Order Determining Alleged Missing Person, etc. is Off Calendar. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order), date of death yet to be determined. Bond in the amount of $300,000.00 is required. Status on Close of Estate set for November 18, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition For Order Determining Alleged Missing Person, Kimberly Portillo, As A Person Presumed To Be Dead And Allowing Administration Of The Estate Of Portillo Under Probate Code Section 12401 (With Attached Exhibits A - H)
Off Calendar Jan-09-2018 Continued To Jan-23-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF DEATH
Off Calendar Dec-28-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Sprague for (petitioner) Jeffrey Rigler.

The Court stated that there is a probate code that can be utilized for a missing person presumed dead. Mr. Sprague stated that the legislature gives 3 ways to go but that the Health & Safety code gives a judgment, that they want the facts of the death out of the way and that this will be less expensive. The Court stated that it has no direct proof that she died. Mr. Sprague stated that no one can find her, that she has never contacted anyone, that her husband states that she would have contacted her children if she were alive, that they do not know conclusively that she drowned, that she could have been kidnapped, that they think she drowned on that night and that they will have the same evidentiary issues no matter how they proceed. The Court took the Petition to Establish Fact of Death under submission.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not Reported)
